title: Nutrition for toddlers 12–24 months
excerpt: Portion sizes, family meals, iron-rich foods, and growth monitoring — WHO complementary feeding principles extended into toddlerhood.
content:
## Portions

Toddler stomachs are small; offer small servings and refills. Expect variable appetite day to day.

## Principles

- Family foods with minimal added salt and sugar.
- Continue breast milk if desired; whole cow’s milk can be introduced after 12 months where locally advised.
- Sit together at meals; avoid force-feeding.

## Iron

Include meat, eggs, beans, or iron-fortified cereals; pair with fruit for absorption.

## Monitoring

Follow growth charts at well-child visits; prolonged picky eating or weight faltering deserves professional review.

metaDescription: Feeding toddlers: portions, iron, family foods, and when to consult your pediatrician.
